zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Houghton於1496年4月28日出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾時，他的父親Sir William Houghton47歲，母親Mary Southworth42歲。他於1505年與Alice Elizabeth Morley在英國英格蘭藍卡爾霍格頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1559年8月5日在英國藍卡爾普雷斯頓享年63歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭藍卡爾普雷斯頓。

nameMeanings.name: Houghton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from any of numerous places called Houghton or Hoghton. Most of the placenames derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, though some have different etymologies; for example, Little Houghton in Eccles (Lancashire), which derives from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn, and Houghton in Bigbury (Devon), which may derive from an Old English personal name Huhha + Old English tūn.

variants.name: Haughton
meanings: English: habitational name from Haughton (Cheshire, Lancashire, Northumberland, Shropshire, Staffordshire), or Haughton le Skerne (Durham). The placenames derive from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .
Irish: adopted for Ó hEacháin ‘descendant of Eachán’, a pet form of the personal name Eachaidh (see Hoey ).
Irish: adopted for Ó hEachtair ‘descendant of Eachtair’, a personal name of uncertain origin. Compare Hoctor .

variants.name: Hufton
meanings: English: habitational name; probably from Stony Houghton in Pleasley (Derbyshire), the name of which derives from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Houghton was born on 28 April 1496, in Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir William Houghton, was 47 and his mother, Mary Southworth, was 42. He married Alice Elizabeth Morley in 1505, in Hoghton, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He died on 5 August 1559, in Preston, Lancashire, England, at the age of 63, and was buried in Preston, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Houghtonが1496年4月28日にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir William Houghtonは47歳，母Mary Southworthは42歳であった。1505年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアホートンでAlice Elizabeth Morleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1559年8月5日にイギリスランカシュアイアプレストンで63歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスランカシュアイアプレストンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Houghton(이)가 1496년 4월 28일 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Houghton(은)는 47세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Southworth(은)는 42세였다. 그는 1505년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 호그h턴에서 Alice Elizabeth Morley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1559년 8월 5일 영국 랭카실르 프레스턴에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 프레스턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Houghton nasceu em 28 de abril de 1496, em Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir William Houghton, tinha 47 anos e sua mãe, Mary Southworth, tinha 42 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice Elizabeth Morley em 1505, em Hoghton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 5 de agosto de 1559, em Preston, Lancashire, Inglaterra, com 63 anos, e foi sepultado em Preston, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Houghton родился 28 апреля 1496 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir William Houghton было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Southworth, 42. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Elizabeth Morley в 1505 г. в местности хогтон, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Он умер 5 августа 1559 г. в местности престон, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности престон, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Houghton nació el 28 de abril de 1496, en Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir William Houghton, tenía 47 años y su mamá, Mary Southworth, tenía 42 años. Se casó con Alice Elizabeth Morley en 1505, en Hoghton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió el 5 de agosto de 1559, en Preston, Lancashire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultado en Preston, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
